text.title: Gcp Iam Design Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns commonly observed when applying the gcp-iam-design methodology. Each pairs a symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.root-cause: `gcloud projects get-iam-policy` shows basic roles on prod project.
antipattern.fix: Replace with predefined roles tailored to the actual action set.

antipattern.symptom: Static SA key exfiltrated via leaked CI variable.
antipattern.root-cause: CI variables include `GCP_SA_KEY` as JSON blob.
antipattern.fix: Switch to Workload Identity Federation; delete the SA key.

antipattern.symptom: Cross-cutting role drift across projects; audit gaps.
antipattern.root-cause: Same role bound on each project individually.
antipattern.fix: Move binding to folder level; remove per-project bindings.

antipattern.symptom: Sensitive role permanently grants full scope; audit and rotation hard.
antipattern.root-cause: Owner / Secret Manager admin bound with no IAM Conditions.
antipattern.fix: Add IAM Conditions restricting resource, time window, or source IP.
